title: The Silo
description: Historic dining facility in a converted grain silo on the UC Davis campus. Offers coffee, sandwiches, and daily specials with popular outdoor seating.

title: Memorial Union Coffee House (CoHo)
description: The iconic Coffee House at the UC Davis Memorial Union. A central student hub with coffee, food, and a relaxed atmosphere. Open to students, faculty, and the public.

title: Shields Library
description: UC Davis main library with extensive study spaces, computer labs, and research resources. Multiple floors with quiet zones and collaborative areas.

title: Bren Hall Science Library
description: Science and engineering branch library on the UC Davis campus. Offers study rooms, technology resources, and specialized science collections.

title: UC Davis Arboretum
description: A 100-acre living museum of plants along a waterway running through campus. Free and open daily. Features gardens from around the world, walking paths, and frequent public events.

title: UC Davis Main Quad (Death Valley)
description: The large central quad at UC Davis known as 'Death Valley.' A popular outdoor gathering spot for students between classes, events, and recreational activities.

title: Woodstock's Pizza
description: Davis institution since 1974. New York-style pizza by the slice or whole pie. Casual student-friendly atmosphere with daily specials. A must-visit Davis landmark.

title: Temple Coffee Roasters
description: Third-wave specialty coffee shop with single-origin espresso and pour-overs. Cozy atmosphere with good wifi. Popular Davis study spot.

title: Konditorei Café
description: European-style café and bakery in downtown Davis. Known for homemade pastries, espresso drinks, and a cozy atmosphere. Great for a quiet study session.

title: Davis Farmers Market
description: One of California's best farmers markets, running year-round on Wednesdays and Saturdays. Local produce, prepared foods, live music, and a lively community atmosphere.

title: Monticello Seasonal Cuisine
description: Farm-to-table restaurant in downtown Davis featuring locally sourced seasonal ingredients. Known for creative California cuisine and an excellent wine list.

title: Raja's Tandoor
description: Popular Indian restaurant in Davis with a full menu of curries, tandoori dishes, and vegetarian options. Affordable lunch specials make it a student favorite.

title: Thai Nakorn
description: Authentic Thai restaurant with a wide menu of curries, noodle dishes, and stir-fries. Generous portions and vegetarian-friendly. A Davis staple for over 20 years.

title: Burgers & Brew
description: Casual American restaurant with gourmet burgers, craft beers on tap, and a lively atmosphere. Popular post-game and study-break spot.

title: Nugget Markets Davis
description: Premium local grocery chain with a great prepared foods section, deli, bakery, and wide selection of local products. Popular for quick student meals.

title: Central Park Davis
description: The heart of downtown Davis. A large urban park that hosts the farmers market, concerts, festivals, and community events throughout the year. Free to visit.

title: Explorit Science Center
description: Hands-on science museum in Davis for all ages. Rotating exhibits, regular public programs, and STEM education events.

title: Manetti Shrem Museum of Art
description: UC Davis' flagship contemporary art museum featuring rotating exhibitions and a permanent collection. Free admission for UC Davis students.
location_name: Jan Shrem and Maria Manetti Shrem Museum of Art

title: Davis Food Co-op
description: Member-owned natural foods grocery cooperative in downtown Davis. Great selection of organic produce, bulk items, and local products. Open to non-members.

title: Mishka's Café
description: Independent coffee shop in downtown Davis with great espresso, tea, and light bites. Known for its cozy atmosphere and long history as a Davis student hangout.

title: Midtown Sacramento Farmers Market
description: Sacramento's weekly farmers market with fresh produce, artisan goods, and food vendors. Easy drive or light rail from Davis.

title: UC Davis ARC (Activities & Recreation Center)
description: State-of-the-art recreation center for UC Davis students and affiliates. Features a gym, pool, climbing wall, courts, and fitness classes.

title: Picnic Day — UC Davis Open House
description: UC Davis' signature open house event held every spring. Thousands of visitors enjoy lab tours, demonstrations, parade, bands, and activities across campus. Free admission.

title: Bistro 33
description: Upscale casual dining in downtown Davis with a seasonal menu, wine list, and craft cocktails. Great for date nights and celebrating academic milestones.

title: ASUCD Coffee House (Segundo)
description: Affordable student-run dining at UC Davis Segundo area. Breakfast, lunch, and dinner options with vegetarian and vegan choices.

title: Temple Coffee — Cafe Study Spot
description: Temple Coffee Roasters in downtown Davis doubles as one of the best study spots. High-quality espresso drinks, good wifi, and plenty of seating for solo or group work.

title: Crepeville Davis
description: Davis institution serving fresh sweet and savory crepes, salads, and bowls since 1989. Generous portions, affordable prices, and a casual atmosphere loved by students and locals alike.

title: Sophia's Thai Kitchen
description: Beloved Thai restaurant and bar in downtown Davis with authentic curries, noodles, and an extensive cocktail menu. Lively atmosphere, great happy hour specials, and outdoor patio seating.

title: Soga's Restaurant
description: Japanese izakaya and sushi restaurant in downtown Davis. Fresh sashimi, ramen, and small plates. Excellent happy hour deals and a lively bar scene on weekends.

title: Icekream
description: Artisan ice cream shop in Davis serving house-made small-batch ice cream with creative seasonal flavors. Vegan options available. A sweet reward after finals or a long lecture.

title: Yogurt Shack Davis
description: Self-serve frozen yogurt bar with a rotating lineup of flavors and an extensive toppings bar. Affordable, fun, and a popular post-study-session treat for UC Davis students.

title: Putah Creek Trail
description: Scenic trail running along Putah Creek through the UC Davis Arboretum and into West Davis. Popular for jogging, cycling, and wildlife watching. Connects campus to the broader Davis trail network.

title: Pole Line Road Bike Path
description: Davis's iconic multi-use path running north-south through the city. Flat, well-maintained, and perfect for cycling, jogging, and rollerblading. Connects the north Davis neighborhoods to downtown and campus.

title: UC Davis Arboretum Waterway Viewpoint
description: The scenic waterway corridor through the UC Davis Arboretum offers serene views of native plants, migratory birds, and open sky. A peaceful spot to decompress between classes or on weekends.

title: Shields Library Quiet Floor
description: The upper floors of Shields Library are designated silent study zones — no talking, no calls. Individual carrels and comfortable seating for focused solo work. Open late during finals.
location_name: Peter J. Shields Library — Upper Floors

title: Kemper Hall Reading Room
description: The reading room inside Kemper Hall (Computer Science & Engineering) is a quiet, architecturally striking space for individual study. Available to all UC Davis students during building hours.

title: Shields Library Group Study Rooms
description: Reserve one of Shields Library's bookable group study rooms. Whiteboards, large tables, and display screens for collaborative work. Book up to one week in advance through the library website.
location_name: Peter J. Shields Library — Study Rooms

title: UC Davis LGBTQIA Resource Center
description: A welcoming community space for lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, queer, intersex, asexual, and all UC Davis students. Programs, advocacy, peer counseling, and drop-in hours.

title: UC Davis Cross Cultural Center
description: The Cross Cultural Center at UC Davis supports students of diverse backgrounds through programs, events, and community spaces. A hub for intercultural dialogue and student advocacy.

title: Mondavi Center for the Performing Arts
description: UC Davis's world-class performing arts venue hosting concerts, theater, dance, and lectures throughout the year. Student rush tickets often available at discounted prices.

title: UC Davis CS Research Showcase
description: Annual showcase of undergraduate and graduate computer science research. Poster sessions, live demos, and presentations across AI, systems, HCI, and theory. Open to all — come see what Aggie researchers are building.

title: ASUCD Dance Marathon 2026
description: UC Davis's largest student-run philanthropic event — 12 hours of dancing, games, and live entertainment to raise money for pediatric cancer research. Free to participate. Come dance for the kids!

title: UC Davis Spring Career Fair 2026
description: UC Davis's premier spring career fair with 100+ employers from tech, biotech, government, and non-profits. Bring your resume and network with recruiters from top companies. Free for UC Davis students.

title: Aggie Film Festival 2026
description: Student-produced short films, documentaries, and animations competing for jury and audience awards. An annual celebration of UC Davis student filmmakers. Free admission — come support your fellow Aggies.

title: Intramural Basketball Championship
description: UC Davis ARC's intramural basketball championship playoffs. Cheer on your friends and teammates as they compete for the campus title. Spectators welcome — free to watch.

title: Jazz Night at the MU Patio
description: Live jazz by UC Davis Jazz Ensembles on the Memorial Union patio. Grab dinner from the CoHo and enjoy an evening of music under the California sky. Free and open to all.

title: Pre-Law Society Speaker Night
description: UC Davis Pre-Law Society hosts an evening with local attorneys and law school admissions counselors. Learn about LSAT prep, applications, and career paths in law. Light refreshments provided. All majors welcome.

title: Graduate Research Symposium
description: UC Davis graduate students across all disciplines present their research. Poster sessions, talks, and networking with faculty researchers. Open to undergrads exploring grad school opportunities.

title: Sacramento River Cats vs. Reno Aces
description: Catch the Sacramento River Cats, the San Francisco Giants' AAA affiliate, take on the Reno Aces at Sutter Health Park. Great value tickets and a fun night out — just 20 minutes from Davis by car or light rail.

title: Sacramento Earth Day Celebration
description: The annual Sacramento Earth Day festival at Capitol Park. Live music, local food vendors, sustainability exhibits, and volunteer opportunities with environmental organizations. Free admission.

title: Woodland Art Walk on Main Street
description: Monthly art walk through downtown Woodland with local galleries, pop-up artists, live music, and food vendors. A charming cultural evening just 15 minutes north of Davis.

title: Woodland Spring Family Faire
description: Woodland's annual spring community festival with carnival rides, food trucks, live entertainment, and a parade through downtown. A great day trip from Davis for students exploring the region.

title: TEDxDavis: The Future of Food
description: An independently organized TED event at UC Davis featuring speakers from agriculture, food science, sustainability, and policy. Six talks exploring how technology and innovation are reshaping what we eat.
location_name: Mondavi Center for the Performing Arts

title: Mondavi Center: Sacramento Philharmonic
description: The Sacramento Philharmonic Orchestra performs at UC Davis's Mondavi Center — one of Northern California's finest performance venues. An evening of classical music. Student rush tickets often available.
location_name: Mondavi Center for the Performing Arts

title: UC Davis Women's Soccer Spring Showcase
description: Watch the UC Davis Aggies women's soccer team in their spring showcase scrimmage at Aggie Soccer Field. Free for students — come support your Aggies and enjoy a sunny afternoon on campus.

title: Putah Creek Riparian Cleanup
description: Join UC Davis Arboretum volunteers for a morning cleanup of Putah Creek's riparian corridor. Gloves and bags provided — bring sunscreen and water. Log volunteer hours and give back to Davis's natural environment.

title: Davis Startup & Innovation Mixer
description: Monthly networking event for Davis-area entrepreneurs, founders, and students interested in tech and business. Pitch your idea, find co-founders, and connect with investors and mentors. Free to attend.

title: Battle of the Bands at Freeborn Hall
description: UC Davis's biggest live music competition — eight student bands compete across genres (rock, pop, hip-hop, indie) for the title of best Aggie band. Live audience voting, food trucks outside, and great energy all night.

title: Birdcage Clothing Co.
description: Davis's favorite indie fashion boutique on G Street, stocking curated women's apparel, accessories, and jewelry from independent designers. Known for friendly staff and rotating seasonal collections.

title: Tres Hermanas Boutique
description: Downtown Davis boutique specializing in Latinx-inspired fashion, handmade jewelry, and vibrant accessories. A community-rooted shop that celebrates culture through style.

title: The Avid Reader
description: A beloved independent bookshop in downtown Davis, open since 1978. Stocks new releases, local author sections, literary gifts, and hosts community author readings. A Davis institution.

title: Delta of Venus Gift Shop
description: Eclectic gift shop attached to the popular Delta of Venus café, selling handmade jewelry, prints, candles, and quirky items from local and independent makers. Great for unique finds.
